title: Garlicky Gooey Tunisian Potato Tagine
description: A rich, garlicky tagine featuring cubes of potato, a hearty broth, and sweet raisins, finished with toasted almonds and fresh parsley for a gooey, indulgent Tunisian experience.
instructions: Peel and dice 1 kg potatoes into 1‑inch cubes; set aside.
In a small bowl mix 2 tbsp potato starch with 2 tbsp water to create a slurry; set aside.
Warm 4 tbsp olive oil in a thick‑bottomed tagine or pot over medium heat; add minced garlic and sauté until golden, about 30 seconds.
Stir in smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, and harissa paste; cook, stirring, for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add diced potatoes to the pot, tossing to coat with the spice mixture; cook for 2–3 minutes so they brown lightly.
Pour in 500 ml vegetable broth, bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Mix the potato starch slurry and pour it into the pot; stir, then simmer uncovered for another 5–7 minutes until the sauce thickens and potatoes are tender.
Stir in 100 g raisins, 50 g toasted almonds, 1 tsp salt, and ½ tsp black pepper; continue cooking for 3 minutes.
Remove from heat, drizzle with the juice of one lemon, and sprinkle chopped parsley over the top.
Ladle the tagine into bowls, serve hot with warm flatbread or couscous.
